title: Pentacalia arborea

This classification places Pentacalia arborea among the many flowering plants that are characterized by their composite flowers. Further research may be needed to determine the specific classification of Pentacalia arborea within the Asteraceae family.

intro: Pentacalia arborea, also known as Tree Pentacalia, is a species of flowering plant in the sunflower family, native to the Andean regions of South America. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory issues, due to its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties. Bioactive constituents of Pentacalia arborea include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used to make herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and extracts, which are often used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma.
